title: List of Subitem IDs on Board returning incorrect amount?!
excerpt: I am running a query to list only the IDs of all Subitems on a board using the Subitem Board ID and getting more subitems than are actually on the board. Have no idea why. The subitems that should not be in the list have…

posts.author: dean_m (Dean Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am running a query to list only the IDs of all Subitems on a board using the Subitem Board ID and getting more subitems than are actually on the board. Have no idea why. The subitems that should not be in the list have a missing parent id (no parent item). Does anyone know why this is happening and how I can exclude these items within the query?

Here is my query:
{“query”:“ { boards(ids: 4126679174) { id name items (limit: 5000){ id # name } } }”,“variables”:“”}

posts.author: dean_m (Dean Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml: @Matias.Monday can you take a look at this?

posts.author: alessandra (Alessandra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dean_m,

Could you please share your response?

posts.author: dean_m (Dean Marshall)
posts.cookedHtml: @alessandra There has been none. I chatted help as well and they submitted email to Support yesterday morning but neither has responded. It is an Open issue and concerning.

posts.author: alessandra (Alessandra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dean_m,

You mentioned a list with IDs, I’m curious to see what that response from our API looks like.

(I’ll also be following up on your support request, no need to respond here )
